  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What kind of review do you want for this section? A) High-level content/claims review (thesis, logic, novelty, structure), B) Line-edit polish only (grammar, tone, concision), or C) Both A and B.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section makes a strong thesis and gap claim that should be assessed for clarity and framing.",
    "R1.2": "The reasoning linking consensus views, the proposed method, and claimed rates needs internal consistency checking.",
    "R1.4": "The \"first first-order method\" and rate-matching assertions call for assessing novelty relative to nearest prior work.",
    "R1.6": "A section review often includes sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements.",
    "RX.1": "We will note uncertainties stemming from the lack of proofs, experiments, or citations in the excerpt.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid endorsing or amplifying claims (e.g., \"first\") beyond what can be supported from the text.",
    "RX.3": "We will confine feedback to reviewing this section rather than expanding into broader experimental or algorithmic design.",
    "RX.4": "We will avoid guidance that could lead to overstatement or misrepresentation of results in research communication.",
    "RX.5": "We will tie comments to specific phrases or claims in this excerpt to maintain provenance and traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What kind of review do you want for this section? A) High-level content/claims review (thesis, logic, novelty, structure), B) Line-edit polish only (grammar, tone, concision), or C) Both A and B.

rationale.R1.1: The section makes a strong thesis and gap claim that should be assessed for clarity and framing.
rationale.R1.2: The reasoning linking consensus views, the proposed method, and claimed rates needs internal consistency checking.
rationale.R1.4: The "first first-order method" and rate-matching assertions call for assessing novelty relative to nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.6: A section review often includes sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements.
rationale.RX.1: We will note uncertainties stemming from the lack of proofs, experiments, or citations in the excerpt.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid endorsing or amplifying claims (e.g., "first") beyond what can be supported from the text.
rationale.RX.3: We will confine feedback to reviewing this section rather than expanding into broader experimental or algorithmic design.
rationale.RX.4: We will avoid guidance that could lead to overstatement or misrepresentation of results in research communication.
rationale.RX.5: We will tie comments to specific phrases or claims in this excerpt to maintain provenance and traceability.
